# Achords

> Multi-agent orchestration protocol for software development

Achords is an orchestration protocol that lets AI agents work together on software projects with shared knowledge, persistent memory, and consistent conventions across your organization.

## Core Concepts

- **Organization Base (obase)**: Sets up GitHub orgs for multi-agent collaboration
- **Repository Coordination (rcord)**: Manages agent workflows within repos
- **IA on CI (iaci)**: Runs agents in CI/CD pipelines
- **KB Web (kbweb)**: Knowledge base web interface

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install
npm install -g achords

# Setup organization
achords obase --org MyOrg

# Configure repo for agent memory
achords obase --repo my-repo

# Check version
achords version
```

## Architecture

```
.achords/                    # Org rules (submodule)
├── .engram/                 # Shared org memory
├── AGENTS.md                # Agent orchestration rules
├── config/                  # Policies & conventions
└── skills/                  # Shared skills

repo/
├── .engram/                 # Repo-specific memory
│   └── config.json          # project_name: "repo"
├── .achords -> (submodule)  # Points to org rules
└── AGENTS.md                # Repo agent config
```

## Memory

- **Org memory**: Shared across all repos via `.achords/.engram/`
- **Repo memory**: Isolated per repo via `.engram/config.json`
- **Engram**: Persistent memory system (https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram)

## Skills

Skills follow the [Agent Skills specification](https://agentskills.io/specification):

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Use when... (1-1024 chars)
metadata:
  version: "1.0.0"
---
```

## Docs

- [For Agents](/docs/agents/) — How agents integrate with achords
- [For Humans](/docs/humans/) — Developer setup and usage
